citations.desc: Attending veterinarian and adequate veterinary care (dealers and exhibitors).
citations.narrative:
Facility representatives expressed that the attending veterinarian's regular visits do not typically include all of the ratitites
and pheasants and the written program of veterinary care (PVC) did not have birds on it and does not describe describe
provisions in place to maintain an adequate program of veterinary care for birds at the facility. Lack of a written PVC that
addresses the care for birds does not ensure that adequate veterinary care is in place. Ensure that a written program of
veterinary care is developed with the attending veterinarian that addresses birds and is in place and is approved by the
attending veterinarian.

The outdoor building that houses Satyr tragopans, peach pheasants, and an Impeyan pheasant, and doves has timber
nails that stick out of the pole frame 1-2 inches up by the roof. This is an area where pheasants could possibly contact
when flying or jumping up to their highest roosts. Sharp points such as nail points could injure animals. Ensure enclosures
are free from any sharp points.

citations.desc: Environment enhancement to promote psychological well-being.
citations.narrative:
The approved environmental enrichment plans for ratitites and pheasants do not contain special considerations for
nestlings, chicks and fledglings to be raised at the facility. Special attention to environment as directed by the attending
veterinarian for nestlings, fledglings and chicks is important to ensure that these young animals are kept and raised in an
optimal environment for health and developement. Ensure that the written program(s) of environmental enhancement
address the special considerations as required.
This is a 1st Re-License Inspection for a Class B license. The inspection and exit briefing were conducted with the
applicant.
All items must be in compliance within 2 more inspection by 7/12/2026 or the applicant will forfeit the license fee and must
wait 6 months to reapply. Please contact your inspector to schedule your next Re-License Inspection.
Conducting regulated activities prior to issuance of a valid USDA license is a violation of the Animal Welfare Act.
n

narrative:
2.40(a)(1) 
   
Attending veterinarian and adequate veterinary care (dealers and exhibitors). 
Facility representatives expressed that the attending veterinarian's regular visits do not typically include all of the ratitites 
and pheasants and the written program of veterinary care (PVC) did not have birds on it and does not describe describe 
provisions in place to maintain an adequate program of veterinary care for birds at the facility. Lack of a written PVC that 
addresses the care for birds does not ensure that adequate veterinary care is in place. Ensure that a written program of 
veterinary care is developed with the attending veterinarian that addresses birds and is in place and is approved by the 
attending veterinarian. 
3.153(a)(1)(i) 
   
Primary enclosures. 
The outdoor building that houses Satyr tragopans, peach pheasants, and an Impeyan pheasant, and doves has timber 
nails that stick out of the pole frame 1-2 inches up by the roof. This is an area where pheasants could possibly contact 
when flying or jumping up to their highest roosts. Sharp points such as nail points could injure animals. Ensure enclosures 
are free from any sharp points. 
3.154(c)(1) 
   
Environment enhancement to promote psychological well-being. 
The approved environmental enrichment plans for ratitites and pheasants do not contain special considerations for 
nestlings, chicks and fledglings to be raised at the facility. Special attention to environment as directed by the attending 
veterinarian for nestlings, fledglings and chicks is important to ensure that these young animals are kept and raised in an 
optimal environment for health and developement. Ensure that the written program(s) of environmental enhancement 
address the special considerations as required. 
 
This is a 1st Re-License Inspection for a Class B license. The inspection and exit briefing were conducted with the 
applicant. 
 
All items must be in compliance within 2 more inspection by 7/12/2026 or the applicant will forfeit the license fee and must 
wait 6 months to reapply. Please contact your inspector to schedule your next Re-License Inspection.

Conducting regulated activities prior to issuance of a valid USDA license is a violation of the Animal Welfare Act.
